2603.26330.title: Mitigating the Reasoning Tax in Vision-Language Fine-Tuning with Input-Adaptive Depth Aggregation
2603.26330.paper_summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on visual instruction data often improves perceptual capabilities in vision-language models (VLMs) while degrading reasoning performance, creating a persistent reasoning tax during post-training. We investigate whether this degradation is related to disrupted access to depth-wise representations, and find that even fixed cross-depth aggregation substantially restores reasoning, suggesting that preserved cross-depth access is an important missing factor in VLM fine-tuning. Building on this observation, we propose Input-Adaptive Depth Aggregation (IADA), a lightweight mechanism that makes cross-depth retrieval input-adaptive, modality-aware, and efficiently parameterized through a low-rank bottleneck. On Qwen3-VL-2B, IADA improves the average reasoning score by 9.5 points and the average perception score by $3.3$ points over LoRA-only fine-tuning with only 0.14M additional parameters, with the strongest gains appearing in parameter-efficient low-rank settings.
2603.26330.paper_summary_zh: 監督式微調（SFT）在視覺指令數據上的應用通常會提高視覺-語言模型（VLMs）的感知能力，但同時會降低推理性能，造成訓練後持續的推理稅。我們調查這種降級是否與深度表示的訪問中斷有關，並發現即使是固定的跨深度聚合也能顯著恢復推理，這表明保持跨深度訪問是一個在VLM微調中重要的缺失因素。基於這一觀察，我們提出了輸入自適應深度聚合（IADA），這是一種輕量級機制，使跨深度檢索具有輸入自適應性、模態感知性，並通過低秩瓶頸高效參數化。在Qwen3-VL-2B上，IADA使平均推理分數提高了9.5分，平均感知分數提高了$3.3$分，相較於僅使用LoRA微調，僅增加了0.14M的額外參數，且在參數高效的低秩設置中獲得了最強的增益。
2603.26330.author: Yiming Ren et.al.
2603.26330.authors: Yiming Ren, Yujiu Yang, Junjie Wang
